_**User (2026-03-17 07:24:28Z)**_

the problem is I'm on a modal server. not local device. How can I Access the terminal of my modal server?

---

_**Agent (copilot/auto)**_

There are a few options, depending on how you've set up your Modal app:

**1. Notebook cell shell commands (easiest, no terminal needed)**

You can run shell commands directly in a notebook cell with `!`:
```python
!curl -I "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anthropics/sleeper-agents-paper/main/code_backdoor_train_data.jsonl"
```
This will show the response headers and confirm if it's an LFS pointer.

**2. JupyterLab Terminal tab**

If you're running JupyterLab (not classic Notebook), there's a **Terminal** option under **File → New → Terminal** or the Launcher tab. This gives you a full bash shell inside your Modal container.

**3. `modal shell` from your local machine**

If your Modal app is currently running, you can SSH into it from your local terminal:
```bash
modal shell <your-app-name>
```
This requires the `modal` CLI installed locally and that you're authenticated.

**4. Define an interactive shell function in your Modal app**

If none of the above apply, you can add this to your Modal app definition:
```python
@app.function(...)
@modal.interactive_timeout(300)
def shell():
    import subprocess
    subprocess.run(["/bin/bash"])
```
Then run `modal run app.py::shell` locally.

---

For your immediate problem though, the `!` approach in a cell is the quickest way to diagnose — just run:
```python
!curl -s "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anthropics/sleeper-agents-paper/main/code_backdoor_train_data.jsonl" | head -c 300
```
That will confirm whether you're getting an LFS pointer or real data.
